summary: Fair‑quality recording of a hot, high‑altitude summit show with mixed performances – Black Peter shines while Hell in a Bucket and others fall flat.
review: The recording captures a summer‑blazing Summit Conference gig where the sound system was uneven (crappy first set) but the ambience adds character. The band’s playing is polarizing: some fans love Jerry’s energy and moments like Black Peter, while others note sluggish tempos, heat‑induced fatigue and weak renditions of Hell in a Bucket, Lovelight and Day Tripper.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is uneven with many negative performance comments, while recording quality is only vaguely described as fair; the mix of high and low star reviews yields a modest overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note a crappy sound system during the first set and variable ambience; no clear indication of soundboard vs. audience source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviews focus on the overall atmosphere and heat rather than set structure; no specific comments on pacing or transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – praised for playing hearts out and high energy, but also described as melting, fatigued, and even walking off stage.
band_member_comments.Phil: Thanked in one review; no detailed performance comment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned positively in a thank‑you note; no specific guitar assessment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent thanked once; no further remarks on keyboard work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey thanked for his drumming; no explicit evaluation of the drum performance.
